item.title: AG Report Triggers Fraud Investigation into Agency Nurse Billing

media:content.media:description: Health Minister Krista Lynn Howell speaking with reporters (VOCM News)

item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well, luh, the Auditor General's report has kicked up quite the fuss over here, with a bit of a stink about agency nurses and their bills. Seems like someone thought they could pull a fast one with some electric vehicle rentals. One travel nurse was charging NLHS over $90k for 81 weeks of electric car rent, even though they never set foot in the province. I mean, you’re telling me those cars were like ghost cars, driving round with no driver? Must've been all electric, eh?
Altogether, we’re talking about $327k worth of rentals and the nurses seem to have misplaced themselves, along with the wheels. Krista Lynn Howell isn’t having any of it and has told NLHS to get cracking on an audit and call in the Mounties if things smell fishy. They're gonna prioritize this business, like it's the second coming of Mary Brown’s. Hopefully, they’ll sort out who’s been sippin’ on the screech and who’s been playing honest. Maybe next time, they'll think twice before splashing out on cars nobody actually drives.
item.content:encoded:
Well, luh, the Auditor General's report has kicked up quite the fuss over here, with a bit of a stink about agency nurses and their bills. Seems like someone thought they could pull a fast one with some electric vehicle rentals. One travel nurse was charging NLHS over $90k for 81 weeks of electric car rent, even though they never set foot in the province. I mean, you’re telling me those cars were like ghost cars, driving round with no driver? Must've been all electric, eh?
Altogether, we’re talking about $327k worth of rentals and the nurses seem to have misplaced themselves, along with the wheels. Krista Lynn Howell isn’t having any of it and has told NLHS to get cracking on an audit and call in the Mounties if things smell fishy. They're gonna prioritize this business, like it's the second coming of Mary Brown’s. Hopefully, they’ll sort out who’s been sippin’ on the screech and who’s been playing honest. Maybe next time, they'll think twice before splashing out on cars nobody actually drives.

item.title: Rum Ragged Showcases Small Town NL in New Mini Doc
item.dc:creator: Zack Power
item.description:
Well, now b'ys, you wouldn't believe what ol' Rum Ragged is up to. They're puttin' out a series of mini-documentaries, like! Callin' it "So Much More To Hear," each one 'bout 15 minutes long. It's like hittin' the road with the band and seein' what that whole shebang is all about—probably more than just haulin' gear and playin' tunes, I s'pose.
Mark Manning, the lead singer, he's been chattin' it up sayin', not only are they showin' ya where they've performed around the bay and all—thinkin' spots like Branch and Boyd's Cove, not just the big places—but they're also gabbin' with folks who’ve heard their music and got stories to spin. They aren't just singin' 'n playin', they're throwin' some poetry and proper Newfoundland storytelling in the mix too.
Seems they're doin' a right job of showcasin' the heart of Newfoundland, not just the music, but where it all comes from. A good dose of culture there, I tell ya. So if you've got a spare quarter-hour and fancy somethin' a bit different, might be worth a look-see. Now, if only they'd perform in my backyard! But then I'd have to clean the shed, wouldn't I?
item.content:encoded:
Well, now b'ys, you wouldn't believe what ol' Rum Ragged is up to. They're puttin' out a series of mini-documentaries, like! Callin' it "So Much More To Hear," each one 'bout 15 minutes long. It's like hittin' the road with the band and seein' what that whole shebang is all about—probably more than just haulin' gear and playin' tunes, I s'pose.
Mark Manning, the lead singer, he's been chattin' it up sayin', not only are they showin' ya where they've performed around the bay and all—thinkin' spots like Branch and Boyd's Cove, not just the big places—but they're also gabbin' with folks who’ve heard their music and got stories to spin. They aren't just singin' 'n playin', they're throwin' some poetry and proper Newfoundland storytelling in the mix too.
Seems they're doin' a right job of showcasin' the heart of Newfoundland, not just the music, but where it all comes from. A good dose of culture there, I tell ya. So if you've got a spare quarter-hour and fancy somethin' a bit different, might be worth a look-see. Now, if only they'd perform in my backyard! But then I'd have to clean the shed, wouldn't I?

item.title: Exhausted but Uninjured: RNC Officers Rescue Moose Calf from Fence
item.dc:creator: Zack Power
item.description:
Oh b'ys, listen to this one – the RNC and some conservation fellas had an adventure the other night in Kilbride. A moose calf decided it wanted to test out being an acrobat and got itself all tangled up in a fence. Rookie mistake, eh?
So, a couple of RNC constables, Trevor Kennedy and Adam Manuel, were there trying to comfort the poor thing. Must've been whispering sweet nothings in its ear or something. Meanwhile, the conservation lads, Andrew Dyke and Patrick Lovell, worked their magic and managed to set the little darlin’ free.
The calf was a bit tired but thank goodness, no harm done, and they took it somewhere safe. Imagine what it must've been thinking – "I just wanted a bit of an adventure and here I am getting the VIP treatment from the coppers and the conservation crew!"
Ah, typical Friday night problem-solving in Newfoundland, where even the moose can't stay outta trouble!
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'ys, listen to this one – the RNC and some conservation fellas had an adventure the other night in Kilbride. A moose calf decided it wanted to test out being an acrobat and got itself all tangled up in a fence. Rookie mistake, eh?
So, a couple of RNC constables, Trevor Kennedy and Adam Manuel, were there trying to comfort the poor thing. Must've been whispering sweet nothings in its ear or something. Meanwhile, the conservation lads, Andrew Dyke and Patrick Lovell, worked their magic and managed to set the little darlin’ free.
The calf was a bit tired but thank goodness, no harm done, and they took it somewhere safe. Imagine what it must've been thinking – "I just wanted a bit of an adventure and here I am getting the VIP treatment from the coppers and the conservation crew!"
Ah, typical Friday night problem-solving in Newfoundland, where even the moose can't stay outta trouble!

item.title: Man Arrested on George Street for Indecent Act, Theft
item.dc:creator: Zack Power
item.description: Ah, buddy, you're not gonna believe this one! So, there was a fella down in St. John's, right? Got nabbed by the RNC over on George Street. Imagine this: he was strutting around without all his clothes—seems the lad forgot some essentials at home, eh? Anyway, he decided to swipe some stuff from a store, and now he's got himself a list of charges longer than a Newfoundland winter. He's been hit with everything from indecent acts to threats, plus a good ol' breach of probation. I guess he thought he'd get a little shopping done in his birthday suit! Wonder if he got a discount for that dress code. Jeepers, some people's kids, I tell ya.
item.content:encoded: Ah, buddy, you're not gonna believe this one! So, there was a fella down in St. John's, right? Got nabbed by the RNC over on George Street. Imagine this: he was strutting around without all his clothes—seems the lad forgot some essentials at home, eh? Anyway, he decided to swipe some stuff from a store, and now he's got himself a list of charges longer than a Newfoundland winter. He's been hit with everything from indecent acts to threats, plus a good ol' breach of probation. I guess he thought he'd get a little shopping done in his birthday suit! Wonder if he got a discount for that dress code. Jeepers, some people's kids, I tell ya.

item.title: Public Review Coming for Recreational Cod Fishery This Fall
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Oh buddy, you won't believe this! Ottawa's decided to have another look at our recreational cod fishery this fall, not that it's a big shocker or anything. They’re keeping things exactly the same as last year, and don't forget, despite them doubling the quota for commercial fishery! Gotta love how they conveniently ignore that, eh?
Our own Fisheries Minister, Joanne Thompson from St. John's East, says she’s sticking to last summer and fall's plan but wants to hear from us plain folk on what we think should happen in the next few years. Apparently, it's all about juggling our need to catch cod for food while not completely emptying the ocean. Who’d have thought?
And if that ain't enough, some folks nearly ended up in Davy Jones' locker yesterday when their boat capsized over by Crawley’s Point in Conception Bay—luckily, folks were rescued, dodging a right kerfuffle there. Anyway, good to see Ottawa’s paying attention for once, even if they ain't exactly hitting the nail on the head, eh?
item.content:encoded:
Oh buddy, you won't believe this! Ottawa's decided to have another look at our recreational cod fishery this fall, not that it's a big shocker or anything. They’re keeping things exactly the same as last year, and don't forget, despite them doubling the quota for commercial fishery! Gotta love how they conveniently ignore that, eh?
Our own Fisheries Minister, Joanne Thompson from St. John's East, says she’s sticking to last summer and fall's plan but wants to hear from us plain folk on what we think should happen in the next few years. Apparently, it's all about juggling our need to catch cod for food while not completely emptying the ocean. Who’d have thought?
And if that ain't enough, some folks nearly ended up in Davy Jones' locker yesterday when their boat capsized over by Crawley’s Point in Conception Bay—luckily, folks were rescued, dodging a right kerfuffle there. Anyway, good to see Ottawa’s paying attention for once, even if they ain't exactly hitting the nail on the head, eh?

item.title: Russia Launches “Largest Aerial Attack” on Ukraine Since War Began, Officials Say

media:content.media:description: VasenkaPhotography / CC

item.dc:creator: Zack Power
item.description:
Well b'y, seems like Russia's gone and lobbed what they're callin' the biggest aerial smackdown since the whole kerfuffle started a few years back. They're mixin' it up with Ukraine again, and I'll tell ya, it's more tangled than a fishnet in a storm. After all them talks, you'd think they'd have figured things out by now, but nope, it's just more of the same ol' racket.
Two poor souls are dead, bless 'em, and there's eight others who'll have a story to tell their grandkids, that's for sure. And get this, one of Ukraine's fancy F-16s, bought from somewhere out west, got banged up while givin’ the air targets a beatin’. Talk about buyin' some newfangled gear only to see it get dented on the first try! Seems like there's no peace on the horizon just yet, eh? Like mother used to say, tryin' to get sense out of this mess is like tryin' to butter toast underwater.
item.content:encoded:
Well b'y, seems like Russia's gone and lobbed what they're callin' the biggest aerial smackdown since the whole kerfuffle started a few years back. They're mixin' it up with Ukraine again, and I'll tell ya, it's more tangled than a fishnet in a storm. After all them talks, you'd think they'd have figured things out by now, but nope, it's just more of the same ol' racket.
Two poor souls are dead, bless 'em, and there's eight others who'll have a story to tell their grandkids, that's for sure. And get this, one of Ukraine's fancy F-16s, bought from somewhere out west, got banged up while givin’ the air targets a beatin’. Talk about buyin' some newfangled gear only to see it get dented on the first try! Seems like there's no peace on the horizon just yet, eh? Like mother used to say, tryin' to get sense out of this mess is like tryin' to butter toast underwater.

item.title: Corner Brook Launches New On-Demand Accessible Bus Service
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well, b'ys, here we are with some news from around the bay. The City of Corner Brook is trying to get with the times and start up a new bus service that's a bit more accessible. It's one of them door-to-door, on-demand jobs, meaning you can actually phone ‘em up and they'll come get ya, instead of ya having to hoof it to the bus stop. Imagine that!
The kicker is it can fit three wheelchairs and a handful of other folks, so maybe now they’ll stop suggesin' Nan takes her scooter up that hill, eh? Assistant Director Aaron O'Brien reckons it'll make things run smoother overall. We'll see about that, but at least it's a start, not like anything moves fast around here! So, mark ‘er on your calendar or scribble it on the fridge, this grand operation kicks off July 1st. Give 'em a call at that Newfoundland number if you’re part of the chosen few folks eligible to hop on.
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, here we are with some news from around the bay. The City of Corner Brook is trying to get with the times and start up a new bus service that's a bit more accessible. It's one of them door-to-door, on-demand jobs, meaning you can actually phone ‘em up and they'll come get ya, instead of ya having to hoof it to the bus stop. Imagine that!
The kicker is it can fit three wheelchairs and a handful of other folks, so maybe now they’ll stop suggesin' Nan takes her scooter up that hill, eh? Assistant Director Aaron O'Brien reckons it'll make things run smoother overall. We'll see about that, but at least it's a start, not like anything moves fast around here! So, mark ‘er on your calendar or scribble it on the fridge, this grand operation kicks off July 1st. Give 'em a call at that Newfoundland number if you’re part of the chosen few folks eligible to hop on.

item.title: Baie Verte ‘Not Ready’ for Economic Boom Warns Task Force

media:content.media:description: Dmitriy Zub / Unsplash

item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well, Lard tunderin', looks like the folks up in Baie Verte are in a bit of a pickle, aren't they? The Regional Task Force is sayin' they're not quite ready for all the new economic hullabaloo comin' their way. Shannon Lewis, the fellow who's got a good handle on things, pointed out that while the area's got a bustling forestry, fishery, and tourism scene, the mining projects are gonna need some extra elbow grease on the community services front.
Seems like the task force was put together to suss out where they're fallin' short and give the government a nudge to get them more support. Problem is, they've got a list as long as yer arm of things to fix – lack of daycare, schools filled to the brim, and no place to put the workers whether they're stayin' the long haul or just passin' through.
But hey, let's just sit and twiddle our thumbs why don't we, until everything bursts at the seams. Seriously though, they're gonna need to buckle down and sort all this, 'cause right now it sounds like a dog's breakfast!
item.content:encoded:
Well, Lard tunderin', looks like the folks up in Baie Verte are in a bit of a pickle, aren't they? The Regional Task Force is sayin' they're not quite ready for all the new economic hullabaloo comin' their way. Shannon Lewis, the fellow who's got a good handle on things, pointed out that while the area's got a bustling forestry, fishery, and tourism scene, the mining projects are gonna need some extra elbow grease on the community services front.
Seems like the task force was put together to suss out where they're fallin' short and give the government a nudge to get them more support. Problem is, they've got a list as long as yer arm of things to fix – lack of daycare, schools filled to the brim, and no place to put the workers whether they're stayin' the long haul or just passin' through.
But hey, let's just sit and twiddle our thumbs why don't we, until everything bursts at the seams. Seriously though, they're gonna need to buckle down and sort all this, 'cause right now it sounds like a dog's breakfast!

item.title: Opposition Leader Calls Health Contracts a “Liberal Scandal,” Demands Inquiry
item.dc:creator: Zack Power
item.description:
Oh b'y, here’s the scoop! Tony Wakeham’s all riled up and wants a big ol’ investigation into some health sector contracts for travel nurses. Apparently, these deals cost Newfoundland a whopping $241 million between 2022 and 2025, according to the Auditor General's latest report. Wakeham’s pointing fingers faster than a saltwater taffy pull, calling it a “Liberal scandal” and insisting it all started with a friendly chinwag at the Premier’s Office. He reckons some ministers were sittin' on their hands instead of doing their jobs. He’s demanding answers and action faster than fish can jump the gunwale!
Honestly, sounds like a bunch of townie drama to me. Maybe they should sort it out over a good scoff and a mug up, right on!
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'y, here’s the scoop! Tony Wakeham’s all riled up and wants a big ol’ investigation into some health sector contracts for travel nurses. Apparently, these deals cost Newfoundland a whopping $241 million between 2022 and 2025, according to the Auditor General's latest report. Wakeham’s pointing fingers faster than a saltwater taffy pull, calling it a “Liberal scandal” and insisting it all started with a friendly chinwag at the Premier’s Office. He reckons some ministers were sittin' on their hands instead of doing their jobs. He’s demanding answers and action faster than fish can jump the gunwale!
Honestly, sounds like a bunch of townie drama to me. Maybe they should sort it out over a good scoff and a mug up, right on!

item.title: Following Mass Walkout, Victoria Fire Department Back to Full Complement
item.dc:creator: Zack Power
item.description:
Well, b'ys, it looks like the fire department in Victoria is up and runnin' again after a bit of a fuss. Most of the crew walked out last week 'cause they had a few bones to pick with the town, but they had a yarn with the Municipal Affairs Minister and the Mayor, and now they're back to business. The Chief, Justin Parsons, went on Facebook right proud, sayin' the meeting went hunky-dory and they've been promised that their gripes will be sorted.
So we're back to full fire services—thank the Lord for that! Parsons is shoutin' out to the folks in Victoria for havin' their backs. Now, let's just hope they don't have to put out any fires with their bare hands while all these promises get worked out. Classic town council, eh? Always a bit of drama before things get straightened out.
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, it looks like the fire department in Victoria is up and runnin' again after a bit of a fuss. Most of the crew walked out last week 'cause they had a few bones to pick with the town, but they had a yarn with the Municipal Affairs Minister and the Mayor, and now they're back to business. The Chief, Justin Parsons, went on Facebook right proud, sayin' the meeting went hunky-dory and they've been promised that their gripes will be sorted.
So we're back to full fire services—thank the Lord for that! Parsons is shoutin' out to the folks in Victoria for havin' their backs. Now, let's just hope they don't have to put out any fires with their bare hands while all these promises get worked out. Classic town council, eh? Always a bit of drama before things get straightened out.
